title: DTOMapper Pattern — NxGN Actions
purpose: **AI Summary**: Entity-to-DTO mapping uses a DI-registered `{Entity}DTOMapper` class with primary constructor, following Capstone's pattern. The mapper has `Map(Entity entity, ...)` and `Map(IList<Entity> entities, ...)` methods (single + batch). It is a pure transformer — it NEVER queries the database. All data is received as parameters. `TranslationService` is injected via **primary constructor**; `requestLanguageId` and `defaultLanguageId` are passed as method parameters. Batch input uses ...

examples.name: Core Principle: Pure Transformer Example 1
examples.code:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ENTITY → DTO — PURE TRANSFORMER                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  Endpoint loads entity (via query)                                         │
│       ↓                                                                    │
│  Endpoint loads additional data (via DTOMapperDataContextService, if needed)│
│       ↓                                                                    │
│  DTOMapper transforms (TranslationService injected via constructor):       │
│    mapper.Map(entity, requestLangId, defaultLangId)                        │
│    mapper.Map(entity, requestLangId, defaultLangId, ctx)                   │
│       ↓                                                                    │
│  Endpoint returns DTO                                                      │
│                                                                             │
│  The mapper NEVER fetches data. It receives everything it needs.           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

examples.name: Decision Tree Example 1
examples.code:
Does the entity implement ITranslatedEntity<T>?
│
├── Yes → All Map methods receive:
│         requestLanguageId, defaultLanguageId
│         TranslationService resolves translations via GetEntityTranslation
│
└── No → Scalar mapping only (no translation parameters needed)

How many additional data types does the mapper need beyond the entity?
│
├── 0 (entity only, with eager-loaded navigation properties)
│   └── Simple mapper: mapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang)
│       Example: Workflow with Include(Steps, Action)
│
├── 1 additional type
│   └── Direct parameter: mapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang, extraData)
│       Example: mapper.Map(action, reqLang, defLang, triggerTypes)
│
└── 2+ additional types
    └── DTOMapperDataContext: mapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang, context)
        See: data-context.md

DTOMapper and EntityMapper are separate files per Capstone convention
examples.code:
Features/{Entity}/Shared/Mappers/
├── {Entity}DTOMapper.cs                    # Map(), MapGridItem(), MapLookup() + batch overloads
├── {Entity}EntityMapper.cs                 # MapAsync() → EntityMapperResult<T> (see entity-mapper.md)
├── {Entity}DTOMapperDataContext.cs          # (if 2+ extra types)
└── {Entity}DTOMapperDataContextService.cs  # (if 2+ extra types)

examples.name: Contract types (the DTOs themselves) live in Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/{Entity}/

rules.rule_text: **Use a DI-registered DTOMapper class** — `{Entity}DTOMapper` with primary constructor, registered as `AddScoped`
**Provide single + batch `Map` overloads** — `Map(Entity)` and `Map(IList<Entity>)` for each DTO shape, with `IList<T>` for both input and return
**Never query in the mapper** — receive all data as parameters
**Inject `TranslationService` via primary constructor** (matching Capstone); pass `requestLanguageId`, `defaultLanguageId` as method parameters
**Resolve translated fields via `TranslationService.GetEntityTranslation()`** — never read raw entity properties for translatable Name/Description
**Include `TranslatedNames`/`TranslatedDescriptions`** on full DTOs via `BuildTranslatedValues()` for translation editing UI
**Handle null navigation properties** — use `?.` and `?? string.Empty`
**Use `EnumDTO` for enum fields and `NamedDTO` for FK references** on full DTOs ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
**Place DTO types in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/{Entity}/`** — never in `Features/`
**Use ALL CAPS `DTO` suffix** — `ActionDTO`, not `ActionDto`
**DTOs are classes** with `{ get; set; }`
**Provide all three mapping method sets** — `Map()`, `MapGridItem()`, `MapLookup()` for entities exposed through CRUD endpoints
**Keep mappers stateless** — the DTOMapper class has no mutable state; it is a pure transformer
**Use [DTOMapperDataContext](data-context.md)** when 2+ additional data types are needed
**Sort child collections** in a deterministic order (e.g., by Name, by Code)
**Map nested translated entities** using the same `TranslationService` and language IDs passed to the parent
**Query the database** from within a mapper
**Use static extension methods** for entity-to-DTO mapping — use DI-registered DTOMapper classes
**Modify entities** during mapping
**Use `async` in mappers** — if data loading is needed, it belongs in the endpoint or DTOMapperDataContextService
**Read raw entity properties for translatable fields** — always go through `TranslationService`

sections.title: Core Principle: Pure Transformer
content: │  Endpoint loads additional data (via DTOMapperDataContextService, if needed)│
│  The mapper NEVER fetches data. It receives everything it needs.           │

sections.title: Decision Tree
content: └── No → Scalar mapping only (no translation parameters needed)
How many additional data types does the mapper need beyond the entity?
│   └── Direct parameter: mapper.Map(entity, reqLang, defLang, extraData)

sections.title: Translation Support
content: Actions uses translations from day one, following Capstone's multilingual pattern. Entities that implement `ITranslatedEntity<T>` have translations resolved in the mapper via `TranslationService`.
| `GetEntityTranslation(entity, requestLangId, defaultLangId)` | Resolve translated Name/Description with fallback chain | Single `TEntityTranslation` |
| `BuildTranslatedValues(translations, requestLangId, selector)` | Build all OTHER language values for translation editing UI | `List<TranslatedValueDTO>` |
**Fallback chain** for `GetEntityTranslation`: request language -> default language -> first available -> placeholder `"!! No translated value !!"`.
`TranslationService` is injected via **primary constructor** (matching Capstone's pattern). Language IDs are passed as method parameters:

The endpoint resolves `requestLanguageId` and `defaultLanguageId` from the `TenantEndpoint` base class (`RequestLanguageId`, `DefaultLanguageId`) and passes them through. The mapper never resolves languages itself.

sections.title: Simple Mapper (Most Common)
content: Most Actions mappers are simple: entities are loaded with Include() where needed and the DTOMapper class transforms to DTO. Translated fields use TranslationService to resolve the correct language.
Catalogue display fields come from the loaded entity, snapshot fields, or a preloaded DTOMapperDataContext. The DTOMapper never translates stale enum aliases or queries catalogue tables on its own.

sections.title: Mapper with Direct Parameter (1 extra type)
content: When the mapper needs one additional data type beyond the entity, pass it as a method parameter alongside the language IDs:
Endpoint loads entity and extra data in parallel, then maps:
if (entity is null) { await Send.NotFoundAsync(ct); return; }
entity, RequestLanguageId, DefaultLanguageId, await getTriggerTypesTask), ct);

sections.title: Mapper with DTOMapperDataContext (2+ extra types)
content: When the mapper needs multiple additional data types, use the [DTOMapperDataContext](data-context.md) pattern. The `Map` method accepts translation parameters and the context:
if (entity is null) { await Send.NotFoundAsync(ct); return; }
See [DTOMapperDataContext](data-context.md) for the DTOMapperDataContext POCO and DTOMapperDataContextService patterns.

sections.title: DTO Field Type Mapping ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
content: DTO fields that represent true enums or FK/catalogue references use structured types for zero-transformation UI binding, but the ownership model matters.
| Entity Field Type | DTO Field Type | Example |
| C# enum (system-owned state) | EnumDTO | new EnumDTO { Id = (int)entity.Status, Name = entity.Status.ToString() } |
| FK Guid with navigation property | NamedDTO | new NamedDTO { Id = entity.WorkflowId, Name = entity.Workflow?.Name ?? string.Empty } |
| Tenant catalogue code | string Code plus display field or lookup DTO | ActionTypeCode = entity.ActionTypeCode |
| Translated string | Resolved via TranslationService | Name = translation.Name |
| Collection of entities | List<ChildDTO> | .Select(c => mapper.Map(c, reqLang, defLang)).ToList() |
// CORRECT - true enum field uses EnumDTO
// CORRECT - tenant catalogue reference exposes stable code/display data
// WRONG - configurable catalogue as local enum alias

Grid items are flat by design and may use scalar display fields such as StatusName or ActionTypeName because they are read-only projections. Full roundtrip DTOs still keep enough structure for save without frontend transformation.

sections.title: Mapping Methods Per DTO Shape
content: Each entity DTOMapper provides dedicated methods for each DTO shape:
| DTO Shape | Method | Purpose | Used By |
| Full DTO | `Map()` | Complete entity representation with translated alternatives | Get single endpoint |
| Grid item DTO | `MapGridItem()` | Flat, lightweight for lists (translated name, no alternatives) | GetGridList endpoint |
| Lookup DTO | `MapLookup()` | Minimal (Id + translated Name) for dropdowns | Lookup endpoint |
Each method also has a batch overload accepting `IList<Entity>` and returning `IList<DTO>`:
// CORRECT — Separate methods for each shape, with single + batch overloads
public ActionDTO Map(ActionEntity entity, Guid reqLang, Guid defLang) { ... }
public IList<ActionDTO> Map(IList<ActionEntity> entities, Guid reqLang, Guid defLang) { ... }
public ActionGridItemDTO MapGridItem(ActionEntity entity, Guid reqLang, Guid defLang) { ... }
public IList<ActionGridItemDTO> MapGridItem(IList<ActionEntity> entities, Guid reqLang, Guid defLang) { ... }
public ActionLookupDTO MapLookup(ActionEntity entity, Guid reqLang, Guid defLang) { ... }
// WRONG — Conditional logic in one method
public object Map(ActionEntity entity, string shape) { ... }

**Full DTOs** include `TranslatedNames` and `TranslatedDescriptions` (via `BuildTranslatedValues`) for the translation editing UI. Grid and lookup DTOs only include the resolved translated value for the request language.

sections.title: Correct vs Wrong
content: ### DI-registered mapper class vs static extension methods
// CORRECT — DI-registered DTOMapper class, TranslationService via primary constructor
public static ActionDTO ToDTO(this ActionEntity entity,
// CORRECT — Mapper receives all data as parameters
Guid reqLang, Guid defLang, ComplianceProfileDTOMapperDataContext context)
.Select(pa => new NamedDTO { Id = pa.ActionId, Name = context.ActionMap[pa.ActionId].Name })
public async Task<ComplianceProfileDTO> MapAsync(ComplianceProfile entity, ActionsDbContext db)
### Batch mapping via instance method vs LINQ
// CORRECT — Dedicated batch method on the DTOMapper class
// WRONG — Callers manually doing LINQ at the call site
var items = entities.Select(e => actionDTOMapper.Map(e, reqLang, defLang)).ToList();
// CORRECT — Defensive with null-conditional and fallback
// WRONG — Assumes navigation property is loaded
Name = entity.Action.Name,  // NullReferenceException if not included

sections.title: File Organization
content: DTOMapper and EntityMapper are separate files per Capstone convention:
Contract types (the DTOs themselves) live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts/{Entity}/`:

sections.title: Rules
content: 1. **Use a DI-registered DTOMapper class** — `{Entity}DTOMapper` with primary constructor, registered as `AddScoped`
2. **Provide single + batch `Map` overloads** — `Map(Entity)` and `Map(IList<Entity>)` for each DTO shape, with `IList<T>` for both input and return
3. **Never query in the mapper** — receive all data as parameters
4. **Inject `TranslationService` via primary constructor** (matching Capstone); pass `requestLanguageId`, `defaultLanguageId` as method parameters
5. **Resolve translated fields via `TranslationService.GetEntityTranslation()`** — never read raw entity properties for translatable Name/Description
6. **Include `TranslatedNames`/`TranslatedDescriptions`** on full DTOs via `BuildTranslatedValues()` for translation editing UI
7. **Handle null navigation properties** — use `?.` and `?? string.Empty`
8. **Use `EnumDTO` for enum fields and `NamedDTO` for FK references** on full DTOs ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
1. **Provide all three mapping method sets** — `Map()`, `MapGridItem()`, `MapLookup()` for entities exposed through CRUD endpoints
2. **Keep mappers stateless** — the DTOMapper class has no mutable state; it is a pure transformer
3. **Use [DTOMapperDataContext](data-context.md)** when 2+ additional data types are needed
4. **Sort child collections** in a deterministic order (e.g., by Name, by Code)
5. **Map nested translated entities** using the same `TranslationService` and language IDs passed to the parent
1. **Query the database** from within a mapper
2. **Use static extension methods** for entity-to-DTO mapping — use DI-registered DTOMapper classes
4. **Use `async` in mappers** — if data loading is needed, it belongs in the endpoint or DTOMapperDataContextService
5. **Read raw entity properties for translatable fields** — always go through `TranslationService`

sections.title: Departure from Capstone: Combined Mapper Class
content: Capstone uses a **separate** `{Entity}GridRowDTOMapper` class for grid row mapping. Actions combines all DTO shapes into a single `{Entity}DTOMapper` class with separate methods (`Map()`, `MapGridItem()`, `MapLookup()`). This reduces file count — one mapper class per entity instead of two or three.
| `{Entity}GridRowDTOMapper.cs` — grid row only | Combined into same class above |
